red_baseline.reason: T027 owns the current/legacy/project/Plugin digest and preservation contract; installer/compatibility implementation belongs to T028.
red_baseline.summary: 1/9 passes; the remaining failures identify current-path, manifest, observation, precedence, symlink, and divergent-write gaps owned by T028.

validation.summary: 1/9 passes; eight failures are limited to T028-owned current path, manifest/observation exports, digest grouping, symlink diagnosis, and non-destructive force semantics.
Syntax check passes for 102 JavaScript files and whitespace validation reports no errors.

review.evidence: The matrix covers absent/current/legacy/project/Plugin, identical/divergent groups, default path, idempotency, force, doctor/install byte snapshots, and a junction-backed symlink without depending on host discovery heuristics.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Every observed copy is compared to the one canonical launchdeck-agent source.
Plugin copies are observations only and tests assert no state/content mutation during diagnosis.
Location kind/path and canonical digest remain explicit rather than becoming trust authority.
Doctor/install snapshots prove no unrelated shared or Plugin state is modified.
All four copy classes, identical precedence, divergent no-winner, and byte preservation are asserted.
Canonical per-file digests roll into one deterministic Skill content digest and symlinks are rejected.
